title: moniera hamiltoniana
intro: Moniera hamiltoniana, also known as Indian pennywort or Brahmi, is a perennial herb native to the Western Ghats of India. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective effects. It is used to improve cognitive function, memory, and overall brain health, making it beneficial for conditions such as Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, and anxiety. Traditional herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and powders, often used to treat various ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
